message:
fix(core): uppercase LIKE/GLOB/MATCH/REGEXP function names in codegen to match registry

The function registry stores names in canonical uppercase (via canonical_name()),
but emit_like_expr was emitting lowercase "like"/"glob"/"match"/"regexp" in the
PureFunc opcode. find_scalar_precanonical() does not re-canonicalize, so the
lookup failed with "no such function: like/2".

Fixed by uppercasing the names in emit_like_expr to match the registry convention.
Also added eval_scalar_fn fallback for "like" in the interpreted path.

Fixes: bd-wa26g (LIKE queries returning instantly via prepared statements)
Previously: 3 LIKE unit tests failing, benchmark string results invalid (95000x "faster")
Now: All 8 LIKE tests pass, string benchmarks will produce correct results.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
